query: html ol vs ul vs dl interview question

competitors.label: Lists
competitors.selectionReason: This MDN lists guide is the strongest canonical source that covers ordered, unordered, and description lists together rather than only one element in isolation.
theirStrengths: MDN is authoritative on how ol, ul, and dl express different list relationships and on the required child structure for each list type.
theirGaps: The guide format is less interview-shaped and less explicit about the production pitfall of choosing list type based only on bullet styling.

realWorldUseCases: Choose <ol>, <ul>, and <dl> based on the relationship between items, not just the visual bullet style. The semantic pitfall is using the wrong list type and making steps, grouped items, or term-definition pairs harder for screen readers to interpret.
followUpConfusion: Screen readers and search engines read these structures differently: <ol> implies sequence, <ul> implies grouping, and <dl> implies term-value meaning.

realWorldUseCases: MDN covers list semantics comprehensively, but it is lighter on the concrete production mistake this question usually targets: picking a list type for appearance instead of meaning.
actionableExamples: It includes direct list examples and structural rules, so the example criterion stays competitive.
followUpConfusion: Our page is more explicit about the follow-up confusion interviewers usually ask next: how screen readers interpret sequence, grouping, and term-definition relationships differently.

actionableExamples: Keep the one-line relationship framing near the top so the page stays faster to use than a longer lists guide.

competitors.winReason: MDN is the canonical lists guide, but our page is stronger as an interview answer because it turns ol-versus-ul-versus-dl into a concrete semantic choice instead of a styling choice.
